title: Enforce structured JSON handoff schemas for subagent returns (context isolation)
source: Anthropic Engineering — 'Building a multi-agent research system' orchestrator-worker pattern (from prior knowledge; live web fetch was blocked by permissions)
description: Anthropic's multi-agent research architecture shows the lead/orchestrator agent should never ingest raw subagent transcripts — subagents run in isolated context windows and return only a condensed, structured result. Currently delegate.py spawns `claude -p` Morty's whose free-text output is fed back to the orchestrator, which pollutes context and makes review brittle. Forcing each delegated worker to return a validated JSON envelope (files_changed, summary, tests_run, status, blockers) cuts token bloat and lets orchestrate.py branch deterministically instead of parsing prose.
proposed_changes: Add a `MortyHandoff` pydantic/dataclass schema in schemas.py with fields {status: enum[done|blocked|partial], summary: str, files_changed: list[str], tests_run: list[str], blockers: list[str], next_steps: list[str]}. In delegate.py, append the JSON schema to the `claude -p` system prompt and instruct the worker to end with a fenced ```json block. Parse and validate that block; on validation failure, re-prompt once with the error, then fall back to raw text with a status='partial'. Return the structured object to orchestrate.py instead of raw stdout.
reject_reason: Apply failed: Evolve delegation failed: (no stderr)
